chapter_title.en: Chapter 22 – The findings (lost property) of the Al-Harram

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/98)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/98)
text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) اللُّقَطَةُ لُقَطَتَانِ لُقَطَةُ الْحَرَمِ تُعَرِّفُ سَنَةً فَإِنْ وَجَدْتَ صَاحِبَهَا وَ إِلَّا تَصَدَّقْتَ بِهَا وَ لُقَطَةُ غَيْرِهَا تُعَرِّفُ سَنَةً فَإِنْ جَاءَ صَاحِبُهَا وَ إِلَّا فَهِيَ كَسَبِيلِ مَالِكَ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Hammad Bin Isa, from Ibrahim Bin Umar who said,

‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The findings are of two (types of) findings. A finding of the Al-Harram would be publicised for a year. So if its owner is found, or else it should be given in charity with it; and a finding somewhere else would be publicised for a year. So if its owner comes up, or else it would be like part of your wealth’.
en.sarwar: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from Hammad ibn ‘Isa from Ibrahim ibn ‘Umar who has said the following: “Abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ’Findings are of two kinds. The findings, in al-Haram (the sacred area), are publicized for one year. If the owner is found, it is delivered to him; otherwise, it is given in charity. The findings, in non-al-Haram (the sacred area), are publicized for one year. If the owner comes it is his; otherwise, it is like one’s own belongings.’”
